compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Option 1 (defensive rotation) LANDED on main via Gerrit change 323 (MERGED), commit 44bafdb9e. LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1. A fingerprinted session now rotates off a session=None pointer; degrade-safe (no-id session) preserved. CLAUDE.md wording updated. Next: option 2 investigation (why a session wrote the pointer with no CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
OPTION 2 INVESTIGATION (why a session wrote the pointer with session=None):

MECHANISM (confirmed, not a code defect): every pointer write goes through start() -> _write_pointer(id, _resolve_session_fp()) (session_log.py:139, reached by append() on first-use/rotation at :169). So the pointer's session field == _resolve_session_fp() at write time. _resolve_session_fp() (session_log.py:47-63) returns CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> REBAR_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID -> None, DELIBERATELY stopping at None (no git-HEAD fallback, to avoid rotating mid-session on each commit). Therefore a session=None pointer is written iff the writing process had ALL THREE env vars unset. The write path is correct; this is environment-or-config, not a bug.

SCOPE: grep of .github/ + scripts/ shows NO committed automation calls session-log/append_session_log/log_session — so CI/cron/hooks are NOT fingerprint-less writers. The fingerprint-less writer of the observed pointer (log 95f1) was therefore an interactive/library/MCP/manual context lacking the env vars (e.g. a Claude surface/version not exporting CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, or a subprocess not inheriting it, or a pre-harness-var session). Cannot pinpoint the specific past process (no env capture).

WHY NO AUTOMATIC IN-REPO FIX: there is no SAFE fallback rebar can invent for a no-id context — git HEAD rotates per commit, PID/CLI-invocation rotates per call. That is exactly why _resolve_session_fp stops at None and the design is degrade-safe. Contrast transition_close._resolve_session (:339-345) which DOES fall back to git HEAD/'unknown' for PROVENANCE (never None) — acceptable there because it is not used for rotation.

IMPACT NOW: option 1 (landed, Gerrit 323) already eliminated the harmful symptom — a FINGERPRINTED session now rotates off a session=None pointer instead of polluting it. The only residual is two DISTINCT no-id sessions sharing one log (both now=None -> no rotation), which is inherent and low-impact.

RECOMMENDED DISPOSITION (env/docs, no code defect): (a) rely on option 1 for the common case; (b) for any non-Claude context that legitimately writes session logs, set REBAR_SESSION_ID to a stable per-session value; (c) optionally document REBAR_SESSION_ID as the session-log rotation fallback in docs/session-log guidance (currently documented only for provenance in docs/config.md:332). No further code change proposed.

compiled_state.description:
# BUG: session-log auto-rotation fails to rotate off a fingerprint-less pointer

## Summary
A session WITH a session fingerprint (`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` set) does NOT auto-rotate the
current-session-log pointer when the pointer was previously written WITHOUT a fingerprint
(`session=None`) — so the new session's entries pollute the prior session's log instead of
starting a fresh one. The rotation guard only fires when BOTH the pointer's session and the
current session are non-None (`src/rebar/_commands/session_log.py:160-165`), so a
fingerprint-less pointer (written by a prior run that had no session id) permanently suppresses
rotation for later fingerprinted sessions.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Session 1 with NO `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`/`REBAR_SESSION_ID`/`SESSION_ID`:
   `rebar session-log append "..."` → writes pointer `{"id": <log>, "session": null}`.
2. Session 2 WITH `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` set: `rebar session-log append "..."`.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: session 2 auto-rotates to a fresh log (`created:true`), matching the feature's
  purpose (prevent cross-session pollution, bug kooky-graft-cap / commit e98a82de1).
- Actual: session 2 appends to session 1's log (`created:false`) — confirmed by controlled
  reproduction and observed live (my entry landed in unrelated log 95f1).

## Fix (option 1, approved)
Rotate whenever the current session HAS a fingerprint that differs from the pointer's,
INCLUDING when the pointer's session is None: drop the `current.get("session") is not None`
clause so the condition is `current is not None and now is not None and current["session"] != now`.
A session with no fingerprint (`now is None`) still never rotates (degrade-safe preserved).
Update the CLAUDE.md degrade-safe wording to match.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A fingerprinted session rotates off a `session=None` pointer (new RED->GREEN test in
      tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py).
- [ ] A session with no fingerprint still does NOT rotate (existing
      test_no_session_id_degrades_to_single_log stays green).
- [ ] Same-session append still does not rotate (test_same_session_appends_to_one_log green).
- [ ] Distinct fingerprinted sessions still rotate (test_distinct_sessions_produce_distinct_logs green).
- [ ] CLAUDE.md degrade-safe wording updated to reflect that a fingerprinted session rotates
      off a fingerprint-less pointer.
compiled_state.title: session-log auto-rotation fails to rotate off a fingerprint-less pointer
